# Transcrição: 01 WHAT & WHY USE THE FACEBOOK PIXEL 

- **Fonte:** 01 WHAT & WHY USE THE FACEBOOK PIXEL 
- **Duração:** 06:37
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Alright guys, welcome to probably one of the most important and juiciest modules in FATC and that is the Facebook Pixel. Now if you're watching this training video, you're probably a beginner Facebook marketer and I know for myself it took me quite some time to wrap my head around what the Pixel was and also how to use it properly or what its implications were for my client campaigns and specifically for the results that I was trying to achieve. So I'm going to try and explain this as simply as I possibly can, not because I think that you are not able to follow along but because I know that it's a meaty topic and there's a lot of wrapping your head around how it actually works. So the first thing I want to start with is to leave you with this. Essentially when you're running ads on Facebook, most of your ads are going to be sending people to an external website meaning it's not going to stay on Facebook. So you're going to be sending people to a website or you're going to be sending people to a landing page. You're going to be directing traffic outside of Facebook. Now when that happens, it's very hard for Facebook to track what you're doing after anything leaves Facebook, right? After the traffic leaves Facebook. And so the Pixel essentially performs three really important functions which I'm going to take you through in a moment here. But what's important to note here is that the Facebook Pixel is fundamental. You absolutely have to make sure your clients are using it. In fact, if your clients are not using a Pixel, if they don't have a Pixel on their website, it's the first thing you would tell them to do because if you don't, it's going to be an uphill battle with all of your ads. So Facebook Pixels are used for anything where you're driving traffic to external websites. If you're using ads to send people to a Facebook event or to a lead ad or to messenger, those are all within Facebook. So you don't need the Pixel for this. However, most of your ads are going to be directing people to websites and landing pages. So 70% of your success is really going to hinge on your ability to get Facebook to bat for you. And here's what I mean by this. The three main purposes of having a Facebook Pixel is tracking, optimizing, and retargeting. Let's go through all three of those. And then I'm going to show you how to set it up and also how to check to make sure it was set up properly.

## [03:01 - 06:03]

So with tracking, this is pretty much self-explanatory. This is the ability for you to see exactly what impact your Facebook ads are doing. What you may have been doing in the past or what you may have seen some client campaigns do is they'll boost a lot of posts. So they'll post things on their Facebook page and then boost it. And what happens when you do this is that essentially Facebook doesn't really know if there's no Pixel on that website, what is happening when people hit that website from that boosted post or from that ad. So you're not able to track if people made a sale or if people opted in or anything like that. This makes it really difficult. In fact, you're kind of running blindly because you're not able to see, well, how much does it actually cost me for a sale or for an email opt-in? So the Facebook Pixel allows you to track specific actions that people are taking on an external website. Not only that, but it helps to optimize. So when Facebook is able to track the people that are purchasing on a website and how many people are purchasing from a website, what it then does is it can actually optimize for more people like that. So it will say, hmm, these people are the ones that seem to be buying. So I'm going to try to direct your ads to more people that are similar to the ones buying. And so this is what I mean by you're getting Facebook to bat for you. It's really fundamental to your success here. So this helps you to essentially drive your advertising costs down and get better results for your campaigns because Facebook is using its big, powerful algorithm to optimize for whatever specific action you want that ad to do or that campaign to do. And it's able to do that because of the Pixel and because the Pixel is tracking all this information that happens on those websites. And lastly, the Pixel allows you to retarget. And this is a term that you may not be familiar with, but essentially retargeting, the best way to explain that is if you think of Amazon when you go and visit Amazon and then later on your browsing websites and you'll see all the products that you checked out. Well, that's a form of retargeting. And that allows you to show ads to people that visited those external landing pages or websites or you could even exclude them. So basically, it allows you to create what we call custom audiences based on people that visited those external sites or performed specific actions, such as purchased or opted in or requested a quote on those external websites.

## [06:03 - 06:37]

And once you have a custom audience, you can then choose to either retarget that specific audience or you could even exclude that specific audience from your campaigns. So let's say you don't want people that already requested a quote to see your ad because they've already done it. Well, that would be how you would do this and you need the Pixel in order to do this. OK, now I'm going to take you through an actual Pixel setup in the next video. And then in the following video, I'm going to show you how to check to see if your Pixel is even working correctly.
